description: A cheerful serving girl with knowing eyes who hears all the tavern's gossip

introduction: *A cheerful serving girl approaches with a bright smile and knowing eyes*
Well hello there, stranger! You've got the look of someone with important business.
I hear all the best gossip working these tables, you know.
What brings you to our little corner of trouble?

options.text: What kind of gossip have you been hearing?
response: *Meredith glances around conspiratorially, then leans in closer*
Oh, the usual tavern tales - who's courting whom, whose crops failed...
But lately, it's been darker things. People going missing.
Started with the beggars and vagrants - folk no one really notices.
But then respectable people started vanishing too.
The Mayor's been beside himself since his own family disappeared.

options.text: Tell me about the troubles in this town.
response: *Her cheerful demeanor fades slightly*
Ooh, you're asking the right questions! But...
The Mayor's been very clear - all official business goes through him first.
Talk to him about the troubles, then come back for the real stories!
I promise I'll have much more interesting things to share then.

options.text: I'm just a traveler passing through.
response: *Meredith's smile brightens again*
Oh, well welcome to Redstone! Though I'm afraid you've picked
an interesting time to visit us.
Things have been... unsettled lately.
But don't let that worry you! The Broken Blade's still the
safest place in town for a good meal and warm company.

introduction: *Meredith's eyes light up with excitement as you approach*
So the Mayor finally told someone about his poor family!
Now I can share what I've really been hearing.
The whispers, the strange stories... there's definitely more going on.

options.text: Have you heard anything about specific locations?
response: *She leans in with genuine concern*
I've heard whispers about that old Swamp Church to the south.
Strange lights at night, eerie singing... gives me the shivers!
The refugees mention it in their nightmares, poor souls.
Some say they've seen figures moving through the mist there.
If you're planning to investigate, be very careful.

options.text: What do the refugees say about what they've seen?
response: *Meredith shudders slightly*
Poor things wake up screaming about shadows with glowing eyes.
They talk about hearing voices calling their names in the dark.
One woman swears she saw her missing husband beckoning to her
from the edge of the swamp before he vanished again.
Whatever's taking people, it's not natural.

options.text: Any other strange gossip I should know about?
response: *She thinks for a moment, wiping down a nearby table*
Well, the town guard has been acting odd lately.
More nervous patrols, whispered conversations that stop
when regular folk get too close.
And some of the local merchants have been talking about
strange deliveries at night - crates that no one ordered.

introduction: *Meredith wipes down a nearby table while chatting*
Any luck finding the Mayor's family yet?
That Swamp Church I mentioned is definitely worth investigating.
Be careful though - the stories I hear are... unsettling.

options.text: I'm still investigating. Any new information?
response: *She glances around to make sure no one's listening*
Actually, yes. Old Henrik mentioned seeing lights
moving in patterns near the church - like some kind of ritual.
And the baker's wife swears she heard chanting
carried on the wind three nights ago.
Be extra careful if you go there after dark.

options.text: Do you think I have enough help for this task?
response: *She looks over your assembled party with approval*
My stars! Look at this impressive group!
With warriors like these, you might actually solve this mystery.
I'll keep my ears open for any new gossip while you're gone!
Just... promise me you'll all come back safely.

metadata.author: Terror in Redstone Development
metadata.notes: Meredith Whisperwind dialogue converted from legacy format to new DialogueEngine standard. Provides Swamp Church location and refugee gossip.
